data.title: Dollar weakness and policy credibility boost emerging market outlook, Morgan Stanley says
data.description:
Emerging markets (EM) ((NYSEARCA:EEM) are navigating the year with renewed strength, as the traditional line between EM and developed markets (DM) fades owing to overlapping macro, policy and market dynamics, Morgan Stanley says.

"For investors, this convergence means that EM fixed income is becoming a compelling alternative to DM. Nonetheless, as this transition plays out, DM fixed income asset prices should continue to have a big say in how EM fixed income performs," Morgan Stanley's Simon Waever said.

Waever said EM assets have outperformed DM across sovereign credit, local currency bonds and equities, helped by U.S. dollar weakness and  corresponding strength in EMFX.

"Still, tight U.S. credit spreads and lower U.S. Treasury yields remain critical to continued strong EM spreads and local bond performance, in our view. EM has not yet fully decoupled,"  Waever noted.

After a brief period in 2021-22 when developed economies were in the front, EMs are once again outpacing them. Morgan Stanley's economists expect differential, historically a key driver of capital inflows, to persist into 2026. Reform progress in India, Vietnam, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia is adding to investor confidence.

Policy credibility has also improved. Post-Covid, EM central banks have garnered more trust and shown that they can act independently, preemptively and effectively in the face of shocks, said Waever, adding, "In addition, flexible exchange rate regimes have absorbed external pressures, while inflation differentials with DM have converged."

The fiscal picture, however, is more uneven. Debt-carrying capacity often still gives DM several advantages: fiscal capacity and credibility, depth of financial markets, and less currency risk, Morgan Stanley noted. 

"This doesn't mean that EM hasn't made progress or DM fiscal positions aren't deteriorating. However, robust analysis requires a careful country-by-country assessment. What remains clear to us is that fiscal positions today are a key driver of differentiation within EM markets." 

Waever also noted that while crossover flows into EM are gaining momentum, the global positioning remains skewed towards DM, with most investors maintaining only moderate EM fixed income exposure.

Morgan Stanley currently has a preference for local bonds in Brazil, Colombia, Hungary, and Turkey and like stances on select sovereign credits, including Chile, Colombia, Guatemala, Mexico, Morocco, South Africa, and Zambia.

Here are some emerging market ETFs: (NYSEARCA:IEMG), (NYSEARCA:VWO), (NYSEARCA:EEM), (NYSEARCA:SPEM), (NYSEARCA:SCHE), and (NYSEARCA:AVEM)

data.description:

data.title: ETHZilla announces $250 million stock repurchase program; shares up over 6%
data.description:
• ETHZilla (NASDAQ:ETHZ) announced on Monday that its Board of Directors has authorized a stock repurchase program of up to $250 million of the company's outstanding shares of common stock, effective immediately.
  • The company said the stock repurchase program is expected to be funded using its working capital and from funds raised in "at-the-market" offerings or other future financings.
  • Additionally, the company announced it now has total holdings of 102,237 Ether (ETH) at an average acquisition price of $3,948.72, valued at approximately $489 million.
  • In addition to the ETH, ETHZilla holds approximately $215 million in USD cash equivalents.
  • ETHZ +6.06% premarket to $3.5.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vornado to buy office condo above Saks Fifth Avenue for $218M
data.description:
Vornado Realty Trust (NYSE:VNO)  agreed to buy the 623 Fifth Avenue office condominium, a 36-story, 382.5K rentable square-foot building on top of the flagship Saks Fifth Avenue department store, for $218M, the company said on Monday.

The REIT plans to transform the 75% vacant space, redeveloping it into a class A boutique office building, complementing Vornado's nearby Plaza District and Park Avenue holdings.

The acquisition boosts its presence in the prime midtown Manhattan market. It already owns properties at 280 Park Avenue, 350 Park Avenue, 595 Madison Avenue, 640 Fifth Avenue, and 1290 Avenue of the Americas.

Vornado (NYSE:VNO) expects to close the purchase in September and complete the redevelopment for delivery to tenants in 2027.

data.title: LM Funding America purchases 164 bitcoin, increasing treasury to 311 BTC
data.description:
• LM Funding America (NASDAQ:LMFA), a Bitcoin treasury and mining company, announced on Monday the purchase of 164 Bitcoin (BTC) at an average price of $113,850 per BTC, for an aggregate purchase amount of approximately $18.67 million.
  • With this acquisition, LM Funding’s total Bitcoin holdings as of August 22, 2025, have increased to approximately 311 BTC.
  • This purchase follows LM Funding’s recent $23 million financing, the majority of which was allocated to supporting treasury growth.
  • The company estimates that the value of its 311 Bitcoin holdings was approximately $36.1 million or $2.38 per outstanding share, based on a Bitcoin price of approximately $116,000 as of August 22, 2025.
  • LMFA -1.45% premarket to $1.35.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Biggest stock movers Monday: NIO, VALN, KDP, crypto stocks, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower Monday morning as Wall Street kicked off the final trading week of August with focus on Nvidia’s upcoming earnings.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Vital Energy (NYSE:VTLE) +17% – Shares jumped after reports surfaced that Crescent Energy (CRGY) is in advanced talks to acquire the smaller Permian Basin producer, with a deal potentially to be announced as soon as this week. No financial terms have been disclosed. Crescent has a market value of about $2.5B, while Vital has a market value of approximately $600M; however, Vital also carries around $2.3B in long-term debt. For FY2024, Vital reported production of 133,845 boe/day, up 38% from 96,591 boe/day in FY2023.
  • Sasol (NYSE:SSL) +7% - Shares soared after the South African petrochemical group swung back to annual profit on tighter cost controls and fewer write-downs. Basic EPS rose to R10.60 from a loss of R69.94 last year, while HEPS climbed 93% to R35.13. Impairments included R13.1B at the Secunda and Sasolburg refinery CGUs and R4.4B at Mozambique gas assets. Sasol increased carbon credit purchases to R723M as it ramps up coal-based output alongside renewable energy investments. The company highlighted progress at Natref toward Clean Fuels 2 compliance with its first low-carbon boiler installed and a second due this month. Sasol achieved an oil breakeven price of $59/bbl and said net debt stood at $3.7B, above the $3B threshold that enables a final dividend.
  • Nio (NYSE:NIO) +6% – Shares extended gains after a 14% surge on Friday, fueled by the unveiling of the company’s new ES8 SUV on Aug. 21. Priced at 308,800 yuan ($43,000) under a battery subscription plan, the ES8 is among Nio’s most affordable models. For comparison, its premium SUVs are typically priced between 338,000 and 768,000 yuan. The subscription plan reduces upfront costs and offers customers the flexibility to swap or upgrade batteries for a monthly fee. Deliveries are expected to begin in late September.

Biggest stock losers

  • Valneva (NASDAQ:VALN) -25% – Shares tumbled after the French drugmaker said the U.S. Food and Drug Administration suspended the license for its chikungunya vaccine, Ixchiq, with immediate effect. The decision follows four reports of serious adverse events consistent with the disease, forcing Valneva to halt all shipments and sales in the U.S. While the company is assessing the potential impact of a permanent withdrawal, it said its financial outlook remains unchanged. Ixchiq generated €7.5M ($8.8M) in sales during the first half of the year, accounting for just over 8% of total product revenue.
  • CleanSpark (CLSK) -4% - Crypto stocks fell as Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and Ether (ETH-USD) retreated on waning Fed rate-cut bets. Bitcoin dropped 2% to $111,144 after touching a six-week low of $110,779 over the weekend, while Ether slid 4% to $4,587 after hitting record highs late last week. The pullback followed more than $420M in forced liquidations of long positions, including $245M in ETH and $175M in BTC, per CoinGlass. Among major decliners were MicroStrategy (MSTR) -4%, Coinbase (COIN) -3%, Galaxy Digital (GLXY) -4%, Marathon Digital (MARA) -4%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) -4%, and Hut 8 (HUT) -3%.
  • Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) -3% - Shares fell after the company agreed to acquire Dutch coffee company JDE Peet’s (OTCPK:JDEPF) in an all-cash deal valued at ~$18.4B (€15.7B). KDP will pay €31.85/share in cash, a 33% premium to JDE Peet’s 90-day VWAP, funding the deal with new debt and cash on hand. The transaction is expected to generate ~$400M in synergies within three years and be accretive to EPS in year one. Post-closing, KDP will split into two companies: a ~$16B coffee business (Keurig, Jacobs, Peet’s) and an ~$11B beverage business (Dr Pepper, Canada Dry, 7UP). The existing KDP management team will lead both units.

data.title: M&A Snapshot: scPharmaceuticals rises on $360M acquisition deal by MannKind
Harrow announces leadership changes, Andrew Boll to serve as President and CFO
Saturn Oil & Gas renews normal course issuer bid for further one-year term
data.description:
Canadian energy company Saturn Oil & Gas (OTCQX:OILSF) announced on Monday the renewal of its normal course issuer bid for a further one-year term.

The company said the Toronto Stock Exchange has accepted its notice to renew the stock buyback program, following completion of an existing program that expires on August 26.

Saturn is set to purchase for cancellation up to 12.08M shares of common stock, representing ~10% of the public float as of August 21.

As of August 21, the company had 192.86M shares issued and outstanding.

As of July 31, the company had repurchased, and canceled, about 9.73M shares for $20.3M. The weighted average price per share comes to $2.09.

Separately, Saturn also completed a substantial issuer bid, which closed on July 16. The company had cancelled a further 1.61M shares purchased at $2.15 per share.

From August 27, 2024, to July 31, 2025, the company has cancelled a total of 11.3M shares, representing a reduction of ~6% in shares outstanding as at August 27, 2024.

data.title: PDD Holdings rallies after battling through tariffs to post a better-than-feared Q2
data.description:
PDD Holdings (NASDAQ:PDD) rallied in early action on Monday after the e-commerce company topped expectations with its second quarter earnings report.

Revenue was up 8.7% from a year ago to $14.5 billion for the parent company of Temu and Pinduoduo to beat the consensus mark by $160 million. The increase was noted to be primarily due to the increase in revenues from online marketing services and transaction services.

Total cost of revenues was up 36% from a year ago. The increase mainly came from the increased fulfillment fees, bandwidth and server costs, and payment processing fees. Total operating expenses were 5% higher due to the increase in sales and marketing expenses. Non-GAAP operating profit was down 21%. Non-GAAP EPADS of $3.08 topped the consensus estimate by $1.02 and was ahead of last year's mark of $2.90.

"Revenues growth further moderated this quarter amid intense competition," stated PDD Finance VP Ms. Jun Liu. "As we remain focused on long-term value creation, the sustained investments may continue to weigh on short-term profitability," added Liu.

Analysts highlighted that the Chinese government's stimulus program helped boost spending in certain consumer goods categories. 

Shares of PDD Holdings (NASDAQ:PDD) were up 6.0% in premarket trading on Monday to follow on Friday's 3.4% rise.
